RJJJ.name: Tokyo FIR
RJJJ.emergency_procedure:
出典: RM Basic 2-04 §5（ICAO Standard）
・Emergency: Sq7700。使用中周波数またはVHF 121.5 MHzでMAYDAY宣言。他機に優先。
・Radio Failure（Sq7600）:
　レーダー空域：最後の高度指定/Sq7600設定/強制報告点未通報のうち最も遅い時刻から7分間最終指定高度（または最低高度）を維持→FPL通り飛行。
　非レーダー空域：強制報告点未通報から20分間最終高度維持→FPL通り。
　IMC時はILS/GNSS等の計器進入→ETAまたは最終EATのうち遅い方から30分以内に着陸。
・Unlawful Interference: Sq7500（ATC指定コードがあればそれを維持）。
・Distress Freq: VHF 121.5 MHz / UHF 243.0 MHz / HF 500kHz・2182kHz・8364kHz
RJJJ.contingency_procedure:
ICAO Standard（2-04 RM Basic §5）
可能な限り事前にATCから改訂クリアランスを取得する。
取得できない場合：
・組織的経路/トラックから逸脱（可能なら離れる方向へ）
・使用中周波数および121.5 MHz（バックアップ：123.45 MHz）でブロードキャスト：
　コールサイン・FL・位置（ATSルート/トラックコード含む）・意図
・ACAS（装備時）および目視で相反トラフィック監視
・全外部灯火点灯（運航制限に準拠）
RJJJ.cpdlc_procedure:
出典: 2-02 2.4 COMMUNICATION JPN §2.4.5
Tokyo FIR（RJJJ）のCPDLC手順
・Fukuoka FIR（RJJJ洋上管制区）ではCPDLC運用が行われています。
・Auto Transfer: RJJJ⇔RPHI（マニラFIR）の双方向でCPDLC Auto Transferが実施されます（2026/02/24改訂）。
・Logonタイミング・詳細手順は RM_JALISSUE_Japan 2-02 §2.4.5を参照してください。
RJJJ.reg_rules:
出典: RM_REGPROC JPN（Rev.26.2 / 16 APR 2026）
空域分類: Class A（FL290以上のControl Area）
速度制限: 管制圏内・Approach Control Area内の高度3,050m（10,000ft）以下は250kt IAS以下
高度計規正: FL140以上→1013.2hPa / FL140未満→QNH
TA: 14,000ft / TL: FL140
RVSM: FL290〜FL410（RVSM承認機間は1,000ft垂直間隔、非RVSM機は2,000ft間隔）
FLOS: Track 000°〜179° = 奇数FL（FL290/310/330…）/ Track 180°〜359° = 偶数FL（FL300/320/340…）
SSR: ATC指定コードを使用。VFR: 10,000ft以下→1200、10,000ft以上→1400。レーダー外IFR空域進入→2000。
CDR: CDR1（AIP Japan掲載）/ CDR2（NOTAMで前日2100JSTまでに通知）

RJJJ-F.reg_rules:
出典: RM_REGPROC JPN（Rev.26.2 / 16 APR 2026）
空域分類: Class A（FL290以上のControl Area / FL200以上のOceanic Control Area）
速度制限: 高度3,050m（10,000ft）以下は250kt IAS以下
高度計規正: FL140以上→1013.2hPa / 未満→QNH。Oceanic airspaceは全高度で1013.2hPa。

FLOS: Track 000°〜179° = 奇数FL（FL290/310/…）/ Track 180°〜359° = 偶数FL（FL300/320/…）
SLOP（太平洋洋上管制区のみ）: 最大2NM右オフセット可。ATCへの通報・クリアランス不要。レーダー識別時はCenter Lineに戻す。位置報告はATCルート中心線基準。
RNP10（洋上）: 利用可能。ADS-C/CPDLC（RCP240+RSP180要件）で50NM縦分離適用。
CDR: CDR1（AIP Japan掲載）/ CDR2（NOTAMで前日2100JSTまでに通知）
Large Height Deviation: RVSM空域内で高度逸脱発生時は速やかにATCへ報告

ZSHA.name: Shanghai FIR
ZSHA.position_report: FIR境界の15〜20分前にATCへ通報し通過許可を取得すること。ただしJALフライトは通常のポジションレポートと交信が維持されていれば不要。各報告点通過時に即時報告：コールサイン・位置・時刻・FL・状況・次点ETA。
ZSHA.emergency_procedure:
出典: RM_JALISSUE_INT 2-03 §2.1.1（EMERG PROC - PRC）
・通信途絶の判断: 現用・代替・121.5・前後周波数すべてで応答なし→Sq7600
・盲信送信（飛行意図変更前）: 全指定周波数+121.5 MHzで2回「TRANSMITTING BLIND, [ATS機関名], [便名], [意図]（REPEAT）」
・飛行継続（レーダー有）: 最終高度指定/Sq7600設定/強制報告点未通報のうち最も遅い時刻から7分間最終指定速度・高度維持→FPL通り
・飛行継続（非レーダー）: 強制報告点未通報から20分間最終速度・高度維持→FPL通り
・出発空港へ回航: Sq7601-7600を30秒間隔で2回交互設定→最終Sq7600で着陸
・T/O Alternateへ: Sq7602-7600を30秒間隔で2回→最終Sq7600
・Dest Alternateへ: ミスドアプローチ後→ホールディングFIXで最低高度→FPL通りDest Alternateへ
・Emergency一般: Sq7700
special_notes: RVSM airspace FL291〜FL411（中国はメートル制：ATCはメートルでクリアランス、パイロットはフィートで飛行）
ATCクリアランスはメートル単位（例：9200m）→ China RVSM変換表でフィートに換算
FIR境界通過時は入る側・出る側の両ACCに報告
AKARA-FUKUE回廊：SADLI以東はFukuoka ACC周波数、以西はShanghai ACC周波数を常時監視
A593代替ルート AKARA-APITO-MIKES：A593上の悪天候回避時に使用可
Non-RVSM機は原則FL291〜FL411の飛行不可（例外：人道支援等、事前申請必要）

contacts.main: Shanghai Control (AKARA-FUKUE corridor時はFukuoka/Shanghai双方監視)

可能な限り事前にATCから改訂クリアランスを取得する。
取得できない場合：
・組織的経路/トラックから逸脱（可能なら離れる方向へ）
・使用中周波数および121.5 MHz（バックアップ：123.45 MHz）でブロードキャスト：
　コールサイン・FL・位置（ATSルート/トラックコード含む）・意図
・ACAS（装備時）および目視で相反トラフィック監視
・全外部灯火点灯（運航制限に準拠）
ZSHA.cpdlc_procedure:
出典: 2-03 §2.2.15（CPDLC - PRC）
Shanghai FIR（ZSHA）はCPDLC適用対象外。
§2.2.15が対象とするPRC CPDLCは中国西部の限定ルートのみ（LanzhouのL888・KADKI〜XKC、Urumqi管轄のY1・Y2）。上海・三亜・広州など東中国FIRには2-03 CPDLC手順の記載なし。
通信手段: VHF音声（主）+ HF（バックアップ）。
ZSHA.reg_rules:
出典: RM_REGPROC PRC（Rev.26.2 / 16 APR 2026）
空域分類: Class A（高度6,000m〜20,000m、≈FL191〜FL660）IFR専用
速度制限: 3,000m（≈10,000ft）以下は470km/h（≈250kt）以下
高度計規正: 巡航中は1013.2hPa（標準）
⚠️ ATCはメートル単位でクリアランス発出: ATC clearanceはメートル表示（例: 9200m）→ China FLAS変換表でフィートに換算して飛行
RVSM: FL291〜FL411（8,900m〜12,500m）、300m（≈1,000ft）垂直間隔
SLOP: 非レーダー空域→1NMまたは2NM右オフセット可 / レーダー空域→ATCクリアランス要
AKARA-FUKUE回廊: SADLI以東はFukuoka ACC周波数を常時モニター / SADLI以西はShanghai ACC周波数をモニター
A593 FLAS（China側・SADLIにて）: EB（東向き）FL250/290/310/390 / WB（西向き）FL240/280/300/400
ADS-B: 2020年12月より義務化（全機）
ACAS II: 大型・重量機に必須
FIR境界通過: 15〜20分前にATCへ通報（JALは通常交信維持で不要）

VHHK.name: Hong Kong FIR
VHHK.emergency_procedure:
出典: RM_JALISSUE_INT 2-03 §2.1.4.1（EMERG PROC - SEA / Hong Kong）
・VMC: 最寄りの適切な空港に着陸
・IMC 到着機: 指定STARに従いターミネーションポイントへ→ホールディング→ILSアプローチ
　RWY 07系: LIMES hold → 4,500ft → ILS
　RWY 25 (RIVMI): RIVMI hold → 4,500ft → ILS
　RWY 25 (LUDLA): LUDLA hold → 4,500ft → RNP AR
・到着機（レーダー誘導中）: 1分間無応答→通信途絶→上記IMC手順に従う
　最低扇形高度以下なら直ちに上昇
・出発機: 最終クリアランス遵守→SID次報告点まで→計画巡航高度に上昇→SID/Transition→FPL
・通過機（非レーダー）: 強制報告点未通報から20分間最終速度・高度維持→FPL通り
・通過機（レーダー誘導下）: 7min間最終速度・高度維持→FPL復帰
・Sq7600。RVSM中の通信途絶機は非RVSM扱い→2,000ft垂直間隔適用
special_notes: RNP2 required on RNAV routes
Busy terminal area - follow ATC instructions precisely
Interface between Chinese and ICAO procedures
VHHK.contingency_procedure:
ICAO Standard（2-04 RM Basic §5）
可能な限り事前にATCから改訂クリアランスを取得する。
取得できない場合：
・組織的経路/トラックから逸脱（可能なら離れる方向へ）
・使用中周波数および121.5 MHz（バックアップ：123.45 MHz）でブロードキャスト：
　コールサイン・FL・位置（ATSルート/トラックコード含む）・意図
・ACAS（装備時）および目視で相反トラフィック監視
・全外部灯火点灯（運航制限に準拠）
VHHK.cpdlc_procedure:
2-03にはHong Kong FIR固有のCPDLC手順記載なし。
§2.2.18（CPDLC-SEA）にVHHK向けの独立した項なし。HKG FIRは高密度レーダー管制環境のため、VHF音声通信が主体。
通信手段: VHF音声（主）。
VHHK.reg_rules:
出典: RM_REGPROC SEA.3（Rev.26.2 / 16 APR 2026）
空域分類: Class A / C / G（管制空域内はIFR必須）
TA: 9,000ft / TL: FL110（QNH ≤979hPaの場合はFL120）
RVSM: FL290〜FL410
CFL遵守: TMA退出点に到達する前に指定CFLに達すること
監視ルート（MNT）: A1 / A461 / A583 / B330 / G86 / R473 / L642 / M771 / P901

RCAA.name: Taipei FIR
RCAA.emergency_procedure:
出典: RM_JALISSUE_INT 2-03 §2.1.4.3（EMERG PROC - SEA / Taiwan）
・VMC / IMC: ICAO Standard手順を適用
・レーダー誘導中のComm Failure:
　Sq7600 → レーダー空域で7分間最終高度維持 → FPL復帰 → 指定NAIDまで → EATまたはETA以近に降下開始 → ILS → ETA+30分以内に着陸
・防空警報中（Air Raid）:
　台北FIR進入禁止（ROCAF Air Control Centerが管制）
　進入済み機：ROCAF指示に従い転進または即時着陸
　121.5 MHz / 234.0 MHzをモニター
　ATC各機関へ逐次状況報告
special_notes: Standard ICAO procedures
May be used as alternate routing to avoid Chinese airspace

RCAA.cpdlc_procedure:
2-03にはTaipei FIR固有のCPDLC手順記載なし。
§2.2.18（CPDLC-SEA）にRCAA向けの独立した項なし。台北FIRはレーダー管制環境でVHF音声通信が主体。DCL（Departure Clearance via Datalink）はRCTP・RCSSで利用可能（ARINC 623-2 / EUROCAE ED-85A準拠機のみ）。
通信手段: VHF音声（主）。
RCAA.reg_rules:
出典: RM_REGPROC SEA.5（Rev.26.2 / 16 APR 2026）
空域分類: Class A（FL201〜FL600）
TA: 11,000ft / TL: FL130
RVSM: FL290〜FL410
ADIZ手順: 5分以上の遅延 / 20NM以上の横偏差 / 2,000ft以上の高度偏差が発生した場合、ROCAF（台湾空軍）による迎撃の可能性あり
SSR（ADIZ進入時）: INT DEP 26xx / 62xx

RPHI.name: Manila FIR
RPHI.position_report: At compulsory reporting points. HF position reports for oceanic portions.
RPHI.emergency_procedure:
出典: RM_JALISSUE_INT 2-03 §2.1.4.2（EMERG PROC - SEA / Philippines）
・VMC/IMC: ICAO Standard手順を適用
・空港着陸時（無線故障）:
　通常トラフィック・サーキットに合流し管制塔からの光信号を待機
　光信号なし → 管制塔横断時に着陸灯を2回明滅（軽飛行機は翼を振る）
　それでも光信号なし かつ 滑走路クリア → そのままアプローチ・着陸
　着陸後直ちに管制塔に電話連絡
special_notes: Standard ICAO procedures
Alternate routing option (avoiding Chinese airspace)
RPHI.contingency_procedure:
ICAO Standard（2-04 RM Basic §5）
可能な限り事前にATCから改訂クリアランスを取得する。
取得できない場合：
・組織的経路/トラックから逸脱（可能なら離れる方向へ）
・使用中周波数および121.5 MHz（バックアップ：123.45 MHz）でブロードキャスト：
　コールサイン・FL・位置（ATSルート/トラックコード含む）・意図
・ACAS（装備時）および目視で相反トラフィック監視
・全外部灯火点灯（運航制限に準拠）
RPHI.cpdlc_procedure:
出典: 2-03 §2.2.18.1.4（CPDLC - SEA / Manila FIR）
AFNログオン: RPHI（NAIA出発前 または FIR進入10〜25分前）

セクター別通信主体（FANS 1/A対応機）:
・EASTセクター（A582, A590, G578）: CPDLC主 / HF副
・NORTH/SOUTH/WESTセクター（M501, G467, A583, P515, R337, A339, B462, B473, N892, L625, L628, M772, M765, W17）: VHF→HF主 / CPDLC副
※VHFエリア内ではVHFが通信主体

ADS-C: FIR境界通過時にADSポジションレポート送信 → 全強制ポジションレポート義務を充足

メッセージ手順:
・Readback不要
・WILCO = クリアランス全内容への同意。ROGER・AFFIRMはクリアランス応答に使用不可
・1ダウンリンクメッセージあたりのクリアランス要求は1件のみ
・フリーテキストは既定フォーマット要素が存在しない場合のみ使用

転送・終了:
・次FIRがデータリンク対応: FIR境界30〜35分前にNDA送信 → 境界でEND SERVICE
・次FIRがデータリンク非対応: CONTACT uplink（10分前）→ WILCO受信後 END SERVICE

接続障害時: VHF/HF音声に即切替。復帰は管制指示で新規AFNログオン
RPHI.reg_rules:
出典: RM_REGPROC SEA.4（Rev.26.2 / 16 APR 2026）
空域分類: Class A（FL200〜UNL）および洋上空域
TA: 11,000ft / TL: FL130
RVSM: FL290〜FL410
ADIZ手順: 最終CRP（Compulsory Reporting Point）通過時にETAを報告。ADIZ内は30分ごとにポジションレポート
SSR（INT DEP）: 2700〜2777
飛行計画提出: 出発または FIR進入の60分以上前
監視ルート（MNT）: A582 / A590 / B462 / A461
PBCS分離: RCP240/RSP180要件を満たす機に対して利用可能

VVHM.name: Ho Chi Minh FIR
VVHM.position_report: At entry and compulsory reporting points
VVHM.emergency_procedure:
ICAO Standard（RM Basic 2-04 §5）
2-03 RM_JALISSUE_INTにHaNoi/Ho Chi Minh FIR固有のEmergency Procedure記載なし。
・Sq7700（Emergency）/ Sq7600（Radio Failure）
・使用中周波数またはVHF 121.5 MHzでMAYDAY/PAN PAN宣言
・ICAO Standard Communications Failure手順を適用
special_notes: RNP2 required on B-RNAV/RNAV routes
FIR entry position report required
ATC may have limited English proficiency at times - speak slowly and clearly
VVHM.contingency_procedure:
出典: RM_JALISSUE_INT 2-03 §2.1.7.4（CONTINGENCY PROC - SEA / HaNoi & Ho Chi Minh FIR）
・初動: ATCクリアランス取得不能な場合→90°右または左に旋回→指定ルートから25NM逸脱するトラックに乗る
　高度維持可能：500ft異なる高度に変更
　高度維持不能：最低降下率を維持しながら25NM逸脱、その後500ft異なる高度で飛行
・隣接トラック横断（ダイバート）: 25NMオフセット維持しながらFL400以上またはFL290以下へ上昇/降下→通常使用高度から500ft異なる高度
・双発機でエンジン停止/ETOPSクリティカル: 機体種別と状況をATCに通報し優先処理要請
・悪天候回避（Weather Deviation）:
　「WEATHER DEVIATION REQUIRED」申告で優先処理
　ATCクリアランス取得後に逸脱。取得不能時も同様の25NM逸脱手順を実施
　偏向不要になったらATCに通報・元ルート復帰
VVHM.cpdlc_procedure:
出典: 2-03 §2.2.18.1.2（CPDLC - SEA / Ho Chi Minh FIR）
AFNログオン: VVHM（データリンク空域進入15〜45分前）

適用ルート（H24）: L625, L628, L642, M765, M768, M771, N500, N892

通信主体:
・レーダー外: CPDLC = 主（VHF/HF = 副）
・レーダー内: 状況によりCPDLC継続使用
・ADS-C: 監視用（ただし間隔維持には不使用）

ログオン手順:
・非データリンクFIRから進入: 15〜45分前にVVHMへAFNログオン → 接続確立後、最初の強制報告点でCPDLCポジションレポート送信
・Singapore FIRから進入: 自動アドレス転送（FN_CAD）またはパイロット手動ログオン
・FIR境界通過時: ADS・CPDLCでポジションレポート送信（応答受信後は音声不要）

メッセージ手順:
・CPDLC対話はCPDLCで、音声対話は音声で完結
・WILCO = クリアランス全内容への同意。Readback不要
・1メッセージ1クリアランス要求
・フリーテキストは最小限（既定フォーマット不在時のみ）
・緊急時（MAYDAY/PAN PAN）: CPDLC送信可 → ATC応答「ROGER MAYDAY」/「ROGER PAN」

転送・終了:
・SIN行き: FN_CAD（自動uplink、15分前）→ 境界でEND SERVICE自動
・非データリンクFIR行き: CONTACT uplink（5分前）→ WILCO受信後 END SERVICE

接続障害時: 「ATC Com off」選択 → 新規AFNログオン試行。継続不能時 → 音声通信に切替
VVHM.reg_rules:
出典: RM_REGPROC SEA.15（Rev.26.2 / 16 APR 2026）
空域分類: Class A（高度14,000m以上）およびATSルート上の管制空域
高度配分（FLOS）: 北→南（Track 180°〜359°）= 偶数FL / 南→北（Track 000°〜179°）= 奇数FL
RVSM: FL290〜FL410
飛行計画: IFRのみ（国際線）、出発1時間以上前に提出
ADS-B: VVHH（Hanoi FIR）・VVHM（Ho Chi Minh FIR）の両FIRで必須

VTBB.name: Bangkok FIR
VTBB.emergency_procedure:
出典: RM_JALISSUE_INT 2-03 §2.1.4.9（EMERG PROC - SEA / Thailand）
・通信途絶の判断: 全チャンネルで2分間応答なし→Sq7600。121.5含む全周波数で再確立を試みる。
・到着機（IMC）: 指定STAR or 最も直接的なルートでSTARに復帰→着陸方向確認
　レーダー誘導中：2分間無応答→最低扇形高度以上を維持しながらSTARに最短復帰
・ミスドアプローチ: Sq7600→ミスドアプローチホールドポイントに3,000ftでホールド→4,000ftに上昇→アプローチ
　または: BKK VORに直行（4,000ft）→IAF→アプローチ
・出発機（全損）: Sq7600→SID最終ウェイポイントまで→BKK VORでホールド（INB CRS 120°、1分レグ、1周後アプローチ）
　燃料ダンプ必要時: 8,500ftで2分→最寄りダンプエリア→BKK VOR
・バンコクTMA通過機: Sq7600。非レーダー: 強制報告点未通報から10分間最終高度維持→FPL通り
・レーダー誘導下（出発/通過）: Sq7600→2分間最終針路・速度・高度維持→FPL最短復帰
・着陸方向確認: ATIS/D-ATIS/ACARS/衛星電話等。ILS・進入灯が点灯している滑走路が使用中
special_notes: Standard ICAO procedures
RVSM required above FL290
VTBB.contingency_procedure:
ICAO Standard
Bangkok FIRは大陸空域のため、洋上空域固有のContingency手順は適用外。
偏差が必要な場合はATCクリアランスを事前取得。取得不能時はICAO Standard手順を適用:
・使用中周波数および121.5 MHzでブロードキャスト（コールサイン・FL・位置・意図）
・ACAS/目視で相反トラフィック監視
・全外部灯火点灯
VTBB.cpdlc_procedure:
2-03にはBangkok FIR固有のCPDLC手順記載なし。
§2.2.18（CPDLC-SEA）にVTBB向けの独立した項なし。Bangkok FIRは大陸・レーダー管制環境が主体のため、データリンク（FANS 1/A CPDLC）の適用は限定的。
通信手段: VHF音声（主）。
VTBB.reg_rules:
出典: RM_REGPROC SEA.14（Rev.26.2 / 16 APR 2026）
IFR飛行: FL200以上の空域ではIFR必須
TA: 11,000ft / TL: FL130
RVSM: FL290〜FL410
ADIZ手順: 航空路外飛行の場合、ADIZ進入の10分以上前に通報
SSR: ATC未配布の場合はFIR進入時にコード3300をセット
CDR: CDR1 / CDR2 / CDR3の各カテゴリーあり（NOTAMで確認）
No-PDC FL: A1 / A202 / L880 / L628 / M768 / N891では事前に希望FLを提出すること（Pre-departure clearance不可FLあり）

RKRR.name: Incheon FIR
RKRR.emergency_procedure:
ICAO Standard（RM Basic 2-04 §5）
Incheon FIR固有のEmergency Procedure手順はRM_JALISSUE_INT 2-03に記載なし（日本発着国際線マニュアル）。
・Emergency: Sq7700。使用中周波数またはVHF 121.5 MHzでMAYDAY宣言。
・Radio Failure（Sq7600）:
　レーダー空域：最後の高度指定/Sq7600設定/強制報告点未通報のうち最も遅い時刻から7分間最終指定高度維持→FPL通り飛行。
　非レーダー空域：強制報告点未通報から20分間最終高度維持→FPL通り。
・Unlawful Interference: Sq7500
・Distress Freq: VHF 121.5 MHz / UHF 243.0 MHz
special_notes: RNAV2 required en-route above FL140 (FL290 for overflights)
RNAV1 required for SID/STAR
Japan-Korea route: QNH below FL140, 1013.2hPa at/above FL140
RKRR.contingency_procedure:
ICAO Standard（2-04 RM Basic §5）
可能な限り事前にATCから改訂クリアランスを取得する。
取得できない場合：
・組織的経路/トラックから逸脱（可能なら離れる方向へ）
・使用中周波数および121.5 MHz（バックアップ：123.45 MHz）でブロードキャスト：
　コールサイン・FL・位置（ATSルート/トラックコード含む）・意図
・ACAS（装備時）および目視で相反トラフィック監視
・全外部灯火点灯（運航制限に準拠）
RKRR.cpdlc_procedure:
Incheon FIR（RKRR）はCPDLC対応FIR。
2-03 RM_JALISSUE_INTの当該セクションにIncheon FIR向けCPDLC手順の詳細記載あり（本セッションでは未抽出）。
通信手段: VHF音声（主）、CPDLC（Available）。
RKRR.reg_rules:
出典: RM_REGPROC ROK（Rev.26.2 / 16 APR 2026）
空域分類: Class A（航空路FL200〜FL600）/ Class B（Incheon・Gimpo・Jeju主要空港周辺）/ Class D（航空路MEA〜FL200）
PBN要件: RNAV2（FL140以上の航空路。FL290以上のオーバーフライト）/ RNAV1（SID・STAR）
速度制限: 10,000ft MSL以下は250kt IAS以下（B Class: 250kt / C・D Class: 250kt / 空港4NM以内2,500ft以下は200kt）
TA: 14,000ft / TL: FL140
高度計規正（最低使用FL）: QNH 1013.2hPa以上→FL140 / 1012.9〜996.3hPa→FL145 / 995.9〜979.3hPa→FL150 / 979.0〜962.4hPa→FL155 / 962.1〜945.5hPa→FL160
RVSM: FL290〜FL410（1,000ft垂直間隔）
FLAS（主要ルート）:
・A593（SADLI〜NIRAT）: NB（000〜179°）FL270/330/350/370/410 / SB（180〜359°）FL260/320/340/360/380
・A586（RUGMA〜CJU）: NB FL270/330/350/370/410 / SB FL260/320/340/360/380
・G203（PSN〜KALEK）: NB FL290/310/330/350/370/390/410 / SB FL300/340/380
・G339（PSN〜INVOK）: NB FL290/310/330/350/370/390/410 / SB FL320/360/400
・A582（PSN〜APELA）: NB FL290/310/330/350/370/390/410 / SB FL300/340/380
SLOP: G597/Y697/B467/Y437/L512（KARBU〜KAE/ANDOL間、2200〜1300UTC）→ EB/WB共に3NM右オフセット。A582/Y782: EB（KALOD→BITUX）2NM右 / WB（OSPOT→TGU）3NM右
飛行計画提出: 出発・FIR進入の1時間以上前
CDR: CDR1（AIP）/ CDR2（NOTAM）/ CDR3（ATC戦術クリアランス）
ATFM: ATCC（Air Traffic Command Center）がIncheon FIR全域のATFMを提供

WSJC.name: Singapore FIR
WSJC.emergency_procedure:
ICAO Standard
・Emergency: Sq7700; declare MAYDAY on current freq or VHF 121.5 MHz.
・Radio Failure (Sq7600):
　Radar env: maintain current FL.
　Non-radar: fly last assigned / expected / cruise FL.
　Blind-broadcast intentions on 121.5 MHz + 123.45 MHz (callsign, FL, position, intention).
・Singapore FIR specific: Contact Singapore Control on assigned VHF; guard 121.5 MHz.
　HF: Contact Singapore Radio on published HF frequencies if VHF unavailable.
WSJC.contingency_procedure:
ICAO Standard
Obtain revised ATC clearance before deviating where possible.
If unable: deviate from track (right preferred).
Broadcast on current freq + 121.5 MHz + 123.45 MHz:
　callsign・FL・position (incl. route/track code)・intention・next reporting point.
・Singapore FIR: SLOP (right ≤2 NM) available outside surveillance cover (2-05 SEA.1.8.1).
WSJC.cpdlc_procedure:
Singapore FIR: CPDLC Available (designated oceanic routes)
CPDLC is available in non-radar oceanic portions of Singapore FIR.
・SLOP applies outside surveillance cover only (right offset, ≤2 NM).
・RNP10+CPDLC logon enables 50 NM longitudinal separation.
Ref: JAL ISSUE 2.15 CPDLC-SEA; 2-05 SEA.1.8.1.
WSJC.position_report: Request clearance from appropriate authority not later than 10 min VHF or 20 min HF before joining or crossing airway
special_notes: Controlled airspace: Class A FL150-FL460; Class B Surface-FL150
Transition altitude 11,000 ft; Transition level FL130 (common with Malaysia/Brunei)
DRO (Direct Routing Operations) available on N892, L642, L644, G334, G579 at FL290-FL460
ADS-B OUT mandatory at or above FL290 (1090 MHz ES; B1 or B2 in FPL item 10)
SSR transponder mandatory in controlled airspace; squawk code assigned by ATC or squawk A2000
Speed: max 250 KIAS within 40 NM of Changi at or below 10,000 ft; 180 KT to 8 NM from threshold
SLOP: refer SEA.1 - not specifically mandated for Singapore FIR oceanic routes
FLAS: Refer SEA.1.3 Western Pacific/South China Sea FLAS table (semi-circular + special parallel route allocations on L642/M771/N892/L625/N884/M767)
WSJC.reg_rules:
Airspace Class: Class A FL150-FL460 (and more than 100 NM seaward); Class B Surface-FL150. Uncontrolled airspace Class G.
Transition: TA 11,000 ft / TL FL130 (common with Malaysia and Brunei FIRs).
RVSM: FL290-FL410 (inclusive). Exclusive RVSM (non-RVSM aircraft may NOT flight plan in stratum). Equipment: two primary altimetry systems, automatic altitude-keeping device, altitude alerter, Mode C transponder.
FLAS (SEA.1.3): Western Pacific/South China Sea scheme applies. Semi-circular (odd EB/even WB) plus special high-capacity allocations on parallel routes L642/M771 (WB), N892/L625/N884/M767 (EB). Consult No-PDC FL tables per route.
DRO (SEA.13.2.2): Direct routing available at FL290-FL460 on segments of N892, L642, L644, G334, G579. E.g. MELAS DCT MABAL (arr via N892); ESPOB DCT ELALO (arr via L642).
ADS-B (SEA.13.9): ADS-B OUT mandatory at or above FL290. Must carry 1090 MHz ES equipment certified to EASA AMC 20-24 or FAA AC 20-165A. Indicate B1/B2 in FPL item 10. Include 24-bit aircraft address (CODE/xxxxxxx) in item 18.
SSR: Mode 3/A + Mode C mandatory in controlled airspace. Squawk last assigned code or A2000; request discrete code from Singapore Control.
Position Report: IFR flights must request clearance not later than 10 min on VHF or 20 min on HF before joining/crossing airway or CTR.
Speed: 250 KIAS at or below 10,000 ft within 40 NM of Changi; 180 KT to 8 NM from touchdown; 150 KT to 4 NM from touchdown.
Flight Plan: File 120 hours (5 days) earliest, minimum 60 min prior to EOBT. Amend if delay exceeds 30 min.

WMFC.name: Kuala Lumpur FIR
WMFC.emergency_procedure:
ICAO Standard
・Emergency: Sq7700; declare MAYDAY on current freq or VHF 121.5 MHz.
・Radio Failure (Sq7600):
　Radar env: maintain current FL.
　Non-radar: fly last assigned / expected / cruise FL.
　Blind-broadcast intentions on 121.5 MHz + 123.45 MHz (callsign, FL, position, intention).
・Lumpur FIR specific: Contact Lumpur Control on assigned VHF or HF.
　Guard 121.5 MHz. HF backup frequencies available via Lumpur Radio.
WMFC.contingency_procedure:
ICAO Standard
Obtain revised ATC clearance before deviating where possible.
If unable: deviate from track (right preferred).
Broadcast on current freq + 121.5 MHz + 123.45 MHz:
　callsign・FL・position (incl. route/track code)・intention・next reporting point.
・KL FIR oceanic: SLOP (right ≤2 NM) on P628/L510/N571/P574/P627 in non-radar segment.
WMFC.cpdlc_procedure:
Kuala Lumpur FIR: CPDLC on oceanic routes
CPDLC logon enables 50 NM longitudinal separation (vs 80 NM without) in non-radar oceanic airspace.
Applicable routes: P628 (GIVAL–GREX), L510 (GIVAL–EMRAN), N571 (VAMPI–IGOGU),
P574 (ANSAX–NOPEK), P627 (RUSET–POVUS).
Ref: 2-05 SEA.1.8 (KL FIR SLOP section); SAS.4.2.5.
WMFC.position_report: Request clearance not later than 10 min VHF or 20 min HF before joining or crossing airway/TMA/CTR
special_notes: Controlled airspace: Class A FL250-FL460 (ATS routes) and FL150-FL250; Class B 10,000 ft-FL150; Class C below 10,000 ft and CTRs; Class G uncontrolled below FL250
Transition altitude 11,000 ft; Transition level FL130 (common with Singapore/Brunei, except Mt Kinabalu area: TA 15,000 ft / TL FL170)
Semi-circular cruising levels in controlled airspace at or above 3,000 ft; Quadrantal below
FLAS: Refer SEA.1.3 Western Pacific/South China Sea FLAS table
ADS-B: Not mandated separately for KL FIR (refer SEA.2.8 summary; no specific mandate cited)
SSR: Mode 3/A + Mode C mandatory in KUL TMA/CTR. Squawk A2000 if no discrete code assigned
Uni-directional routing scheme along western Peninsular Malaysia (SEA.10.3.1)
WMFC.reg_rules:
Airspace Class: Class A FL250-FL460 (FIR/ATS routes), FL150-FL250 (ATS routes/TMAs); Class B 10,000 ft-FL150; Class C below 10,000 ft and CTRs/ATZs. Class G uncontrolled below FL250.
Transition: TA 11,000 ft / TL FL130. Exception: Mt Kinabalu area (Kota Kinabalu FIR) TA 15,000 ft / TL FL170.
RVSM: FL290-FL410 (inclusive). Exclusive RVSM. Additional requirement: one altitude reporting transponder (Mode C) required in Malaysian FIR within RVSM stratum.
FLAS (SEA.1.3): Western Pacific/South China Sea FLAS applies. Consult No-PDC FL tables for each route (e.g. L625 EB FL310/320/350/360/390/400; L759/N571/M770/P628 WB FL280; N884/M771 EB FL310/320/350/360/390/400; L642 WB FL310/320/350/360/390/400).
SSR (SEA.10.6): Mode 3/A + Mode C mandatory in KUL TMA/CTR. International DEP codes: 2100-2177, 2600-2677, 5400-5477. Transit aircraft retain assigned code or squawk A2000.
Routing (SEA.10.3): Uni-directional routing scheme in effect along western Peninsular Malaysia. Specific FPL routes required per origin/destination pair — see SEA.10.3.1 tables.
Enroute Ops (SEA.10.5): IFR aircraft must request clearance not later than 10 min VHF or 20 min HF before joining/crossing airways, TMAs or CTRs.
Cruising Level (SEA.10.2.3): Semi-circular at or above 3,000 ft in controlled airspace; Quadrantal otherwise.
Speed (SEA.10.2.4): Plan top of descent at 270 KIAS unless restricted by ATC.
Flight Plan (SEA.10.4): Submit at least 60 min before departure.

WBFC.name: Kota Kinabalu FIR
WBFC.emergency_procedure:
ICAO Standard
・Emergency: Sq7700; declare MAYDAY on current freq or VHF 121.5 MHz.
・Radio Failure (Sq7600):
　Radar env: maintain current FL.
　Non-radar: fly last assigned / expected / cruise FL.
　Blind-broadcast intentions on 121.5 MHz + 123.45 MHz (callsign, FL, position, intention).
・Kota Kinabalu FIR specific: Contact Kota Kinabalu Control on VHF; guard 121.5 MHz.
　HF backup when beyond VHF range.
WBFC.contingency_procedure:
ICAO Standard
Obtain revised ATC clearance before deviating where possible.
If unable: deviate from track (right preferred).
Broadcast on current freq + 121.5 MHz + 123.45 MHz:
　callsign・FL・position (incl. route/track code)・intention・next reporting point.
・Kota Kinabalu FIR: Oceanic contingency – broadcast on current freq + 121.5 + 123.45 MHz.
WBFC.cpdlc_procedure:
Kota Kinabalu FIR: CPDLC limited availability
VHF radar coverage in most of the FIR. CPDLC not routinely required.
RNP10 operations use procedural/radar separation (10 min or 50 NM with DCPC).
HF communication used in remote oceanic portions east of Borneo.
WBFC.position_report: Request clearance not later than 10 min VHF or 20 min HF before joining or crossing airway/TMA/CTR
special_notes: Same airspace classification as Kuala Lumpur FIR (SEA.10.1): Class A FL250-FL460; Class B 10,000-FL150; Class C CTRs/ATZs; Class G uncontrolled below FL250
Transition altitude 11,000 ft / Transition level FL130 in general; EXCEPTION: within 10 NM of Mt Kinabalu — TA 15,000 ft / TL FL170
RVSM FL290-FL410 inclusive; Mode C transponder required in RVSM stratum
SSR: Kota Kinabalu FIR international DEP codes: 2001-2077, 0400-0477
FLAS: Western Pacific/South China Sea FLAS applies (SEA.1.3)
Covers East Malaysia (Sabah/Sarawak/Brunei airspace); separate FIR from Kuala Lumpur FIR
WBFC.reg_rules:
Airspace Class: Same classification as Kuala Lumpur FIR — Class A FL250-FL460 (FIR/ATS routes), FL150-FL250 (ATS routes/TMAs); Class B 10,000 ft-FL150; Class C CTRs/ATZs; Class G uncontrolled below FL250.
Transition: TA 11,000 ft / TL FL130. CRITICAL EXCEPTION: within 10 NM radius of Mt Kinabalu — TA 15,000 ft (4,570 m) / TL FL170.
RVSM: FL290-FL410 (inclusive). Exclusive RVSM. Mode C transponder required in RVSM stratum (Malaysian FIR requirement per SEA.1.4).
FLAS (SEA.1.3): Western Pacific/South China Sea FLAS applies. Key routes: A341/B592/R223/M522 EB FL310/350/390, WB FL320/360/400; G334/G580/M758/M759/M761 EB FL270/290/330, WB FL280/300/340; M754/M522(VJN-VINIK) NB FL300/340/380, SB FL290/330/370/410; P648 EB FL310/350/390, WB FL320/360/380/400.
SSR (SEA.10.6): International DEP codes: 2001-2077, 0400-0477. Transit aircraft retain assigned code or squawk A2000.
Enroute Ops (SEA.10.5): IFR aircraft must request clearance not later than 10 min VHF or 20 min HF before joining/crossing airways/TMAs/CTRs.
Cruising Level (SEA.10.2.3): Semi-circular at or above 3,000 ft in controlled airspace; Quadrantal otherwise.

WIIF.name: Jakarta FIR
WIIF.emergency_procedure:
ICAO Standard
・Emergency: Sq7700; declare MAYDAY on current freq or VHF 121.5 MHz.
・Radio Failure (Sq7600):
　Radar env: maintain current FL.
　Non-radar: fly last assigned / expected / cruise FL.
　Blind-broadcast intentions on 121.5 MHz + 123.45 MHz (callsign, FL, position, intention).
・Jakarta FIR specific: Contact Jakarta Control on VHF; guard 121.5 MHz.
　HF communication required in remote oceanic airspace.
WIIF.contingency_procedure:
ICAO Standard
Obtain revised ATC clearance before deviating where possible.
If unable: deviate from track (right preferred).
Broadcast on current freq + 121.5 MHz + 123.45 MHz:
　callsign・FL・position (incl. route/track code)・intention・next reporting point.
・Jakarta FIR: Weather radar recommended for navigation; unreliable NAVAIDs in some sectors.
WIIF.cpdlc_procedure:
Jakarta FIR: CPDLC not routinely required
VHF primary in most of the FIR. HF used in remote oceanic portions.
RNP10 procedural separation applies (10 min / 80 NM). CPDLC not mandated.
Note: Weather radar recommended due to unreliable NAVAIDs in some areas (2-05 SEA).
special_notes: Includes both Jakarta FIR (WIIF) and Ujung Pandang FIR (WAAF) — both have same ADS-B mandate
Airspace: Class A CTA/UTA FL250-FL460; Class B CTR; Class C TMA/ATZ; Class F/G remaining
Cruising levels: semi-circular at or above FL200; Quadrantal at or above 3,000 ft but below FL200
ADS-B OUT mandatory at or above FL290 (Mode S + GNSS source position; E, L, B1 or B2 in FPL item 10)
ADS-B for ATC separation service also implemented FL245-FL600 in Jakarta UCA and Ujung Pandang UCA
Company note: Use weather radar for navigation in Jakarta FIR — NAVAIDs, A/G comms and ATS unsatisfactory
Transition altitude: 11,000 ft; Transition level: FL130
FLAS: Western Pacific/South China Sea FLAS applies (SEA.1.3)
WIIF.reg_rules:
Airspace Class (SEA.8.1): Class A CTA/UTA FL250-FL460; Class B CTR; Class C TMA/ATZ; Class F/G FIR/UIR and unattended aerodromes.
Transition (SEA.8.2.2): TA 11,000 ft / TL FL130 (specified per aerodrome).
RVSM: FL290-FL410 (inclusive). Exclusive RVSM (Jakarta FIR listed in Western Pacific/SEA RVSM table).
Cruising Levels (SEA.8.2.3): Semi-circular at or above FL200; Quadrantal at or above 3,000 ft but below FL200.
FLAS (SEA.1.3): Western Pacific/South China Sea FLAS applies — semi-circular plus special parallel route allocations.
ADS-B (SEA.8.7): ADS-B OUT mandatory at or above FL290 in Jakarta FIR and Ujung Pandang FIR. Equipment: Mode S transponder + GNSS source position. Indicate E, L, B1 or B2 in FPL item 10. ADS-B separation service also active in Jakarta UCA and Ujung Pandang UCA (FL245-FL600).
IFR Requirement (SEA.8.2.1): IFR required for overwater flight more than 10 NM from land or for more than one hour; above FL150 in controlled airspace; above FL200 in uncontrolled airspace.
Speed (SEA.8.2.4): Max 250 KIAS below 10,000 ft MSL; max 200 KIAS at or below 2,500 ft within 4 NM of Class C/D primary airport.
Company Procedure (SEA.8.3): Use weather radar for navigation — NAVAIDs, A/G communications and ATS are unsatisfactory in Jakarta FIR.

VLVT.name: Vientiane FIR
VLVT.emergency_procedure:
ICAO Standard
・Emergency: Sq7700; declare MAYDAY on current freq or VHF 121.5 MHz.
・Radio Failure (Sq7600):
　Radar env: maintain current FL.
　Non-radar: fly last assigned / expected / cruise FL.
　Blind-broadcast intentions on 121.5 MHz + 123.45 MHz (callsign, FL, position, intention).
・Vientiane FIR specific: Contact Vientiane ACC on VHF; guard 121.5 MHz.
　Transfer control applies at FIR boundary waypoints.
VLVT.contingency_procedure:
ICAO Standard
Obtain revised ATC clearance before deviating where possible.
If unable: deviate from track (right preferred).
Broadcast on current freq + 121.5 MHz + 123.45 MHz:
　callsign・FL・position (incl. route/track code)・intention・next reporting point.
・Vientiane FIR: Transfer of control effective at FIR boundary waypoints.
VLVT.cpdlc_procedure:
Vientiane FIR: CPDLC not routinely available
VHF-based FIR with radar coverage on designated routes. CPDLC not in use.
Transfer of control at FIR boundaries is effective at boundary waypoints.
HF backup for communication gaps.
VLVT.position_report: Transfer of control at FIR boundary waypoints; standard IFR position reports required
special_notes: Airspace: Class A upper ATS routes FL200 and above; Class C TMAs/CTRs and lower ATS routes; Class F remaining
RVSM FL290-FL410 inclusive (listed in Western Pacific/SEA RVSM table)
Transfer of control at FIR boundary waypoints on respective boundaries
Flight plan must be filed at least 60 minutes before departure
FLAS: Western Pacific/South China Sea FLAS applies (SEA.1.3) — A1 EB FL290/330/370/390/410, WB FL300/340/380/400
VLVT.reg_rules:
Airspace Class (SEA.9.1): Class A upper ATS routes FL200 and above; Class C all TMAs, CTRs and lower ATS routes; Class F all remaining airspace.
RVSM: FL290-FL410 (inclusive). Listed in Western Pacific/SEA exclusive RVSM airspace (non-RVSM aircraft may NOT flight plan in stratum).
FLAS (SEA.1.3): Western Pacific/South China Sea FLAS applies. Key route A1: EB FL290/330/370/390/410, WB FL300/340/380/400. Route A202 EB FL290/330/370/390/410, WB FL300/340/380/400. Consult No-PDC FL tables per route.
Control Transfer (SEA.9.2): Transfer of control between Vientiane ACC and adjacent ACCs is effective at boundary waypoints on respective FIR boundaries.
Flight Plan (SEA.9.3): Must be submitted at least 60 minutes before departure for flights requiring ATC service.
CPDLC/ADS-C: Not implemented — procedural separation applies.

VYYF.name: Yangon FIR
VYYF.emergency_procedure:
ICAO Standard
・Emergency: Sq7700; declare MAYDAY on current freq or VHF 121.5 MHz.
・Radio Failure (Sq7600):
　Radar env: maintain current FL.
　Non-radar: fly last assigned / expected / cruise FL.
　Blind-broadcast intentions on 121.5 MHz + 123.45 MHz (callsign, FL, position, intention).
・Yangon FIR specific: Contact Yangon ACC on VHF; guard 121.5 MHz.
　123.45 MHz air-to-air if unable to contact ATC.
VYYF.contingency_procedure:
ICAO Standard
Obtain revised ATC clearance before deviating where possible.
If unable: deviate from track (right preferred).
Broadcast on current freq + 121.5 MHz + 123.45 MHz:
　callsign・FL・position (incl. route/track code)・intention・next reporting point.
・Yangon FIR: 123.45 MHz air-to-air coordination strongly recommended for conflicting traffic.
VYYF.cpdlc_procedure:
Yangon FIR: CPDLC not routinely available
VHF-based FIR. CPDLC not implemented. HF used for areas beyond VHF range.
RVSM applicable FL290–FL410 (2-05 SEA). FLAS applied on L759, M770.
special_notes: Airspace: Class A FIR ATS routes and TMAs FL150-FL560; Class B TMA GND-FL150 and APCH zone; Class C CTRs; Class D; Class E uncontrolled GND-1,500 ft
FL transition: China metric to ICAO RVSM on A599/A201 at positions notified by NOTAM
ADIZ: All aircraft operating to/through/within ADIZ must obtain Air Defence Clearance (ADC) through ATCC
SLOP: Applicable on A201/A599/B465/R203/L301/P762/M770/L759 — 1 or 2 NM right (non-radar); requires ATC approval in radar airspace
ADS-B OUT mandatory at or above FL250 (SEA.11.7)
SSR: Squawk last assigned code or code 2000 upon entering Yangon FIR
HF available: Yangon Radio on HF for weather deviations and communications
VYYF.reg_rules:
Airspace Class (SEA.11.1): Class A: FIR ATS routes FL150-FL560, TMA FL150-FL560; Class B: TMA GND-FL150 and APCH zone; Class C: CTRs; Class D; Class E: uncontrolled GND-1,500 ft.
IFR Required (SEA.11.2.1): Above FL150; more than 100 NM seaward in controlled airspace; more than 20 NM seaward for more than 1 hour.
RVSM: FL290-FL410. Exclusive RVSM (listed in Western Pacific/SEA RVSM table).
FLAS (SAS.1.2.5): L759/M770: WB FL280/320/340/360/380/400, EB FL310/330/350/370/390/410. L301/N895/P762: WB FL300/FL360, EB all RVSM FLs.
FL Transition (SAS.1.3.2): China metric ↔ ICAO RVSM transition on A599/A201 at positions notified by NOTAM. Conversion table: WB FL380↔12000M, FL360↔10800M, FL320↔9600M, FL280↔8400M; EB FL410↔12600M, FL370↔11400M, FL330↔10200M, FL290↔9000M.
ADIZ (SEA.11.2.4): ADC required from ATCC. Eastbound: request ADC on first contact at FIR boundary, provide ETO at ADIZ boundary. Westbound: request ADC only on first contact at FIR boundary.
SLOP (SEA.11.6 / SAS.1.11.3): Applicable on A201/A599/B465/R203/L301/P762/M770/L759. Offset 1 or 2 NM right of centerline. Auto offset capability required. No ATC notification needed in non-radar airspace; approval required in radar airspace.
ADS-B (SEA.11.7): ADS-B OUT mandatory at or above FL250. 7 ADS-B stations providing coverage up to 200 NM. Monitoring service for flight safety.
SSR (SEA.11.2.5): Squawk last assigned code or code 2000 upon entering Yangon FIR.
Weather Deviation (SEA.11.5): Contact Yangon Radio (HF) or Yangon Control (VHF). State: aircraft ID, direction (right/left), degrees, duration in minutes.
Transition Altitude: Specified per aerodrome. Cruising Level: Semi-circular.

VIDF.name: Delhi FIR
VIDF.emergency_procedure:
ICAO Standard
・Emergency: Sq7700; declare MAYDAY on current freq or VHF 121.5 MHz.
・Radio Failure (Sq7600):
　Radar env: maintain current FL.
　Non-radar: fly last assigned / expected / cruise FL.
　Blind-broadcast intentions on 121.5 MHz + 123.45 MHz (callsign, FL, position, intention).
・Delhi FIR specific: Fuel emergency phrase: 'MAYDAY MAYDAY MAYDAY FUEL' or 'MINIMUM FUEL'.
　'Low fuel' / 'Fuel emergency' phrases NOT recognized (DGCA CAR SECN 8).
　If diversion anticipated: notify ATC ≥10 min prior with alternate name (2-05 SAS.4.2.7).
VIDF.contingency_procedure:
ICAO Standard
Obtain revised ATC clearance before deviating where possible.
If unable: deviate from track (right preferred).
Broadcast on current freq + 121.5 MHz + 123.45 MHz:
　callsign・FL・position (incl. route/track code)・intention・next reporting point.
・Delhi FIR: Air Defence Clearance (ADC) required for ADIZ entry; obtain ≥10 min prior (SAS.4.4).
VIDF.cpdlc_procedure:
Delhi FIR: CPDLC via ADS-CPDLC available
All flights entering Delhi FIR (excl. from Mumbai/Kolkata FIR) shall forward estimates
for FIR boundary (FL + SSR code) ≥10 min prior via VHF, HF, or ADS-CPDLC.
RNP2/RNP4 + ADS-B OUT + RCP240 aircraft may use CPDLC for 20 NM Space-Based ADS-B
separation on L639 (RASKI–RITPO) and L301 (RASKI–ANKOX).
HF frequencies: 10018 kHz (day primary), 5658 kHz (night primary), 3467 kHz (night secondary).
Ref: 2-05 SAS.4.3 / SAS.4.2.6.
VIDF.position_report: All flights entering Delhi FIR (excluding from Mumbai or Kolkata FIR) must forward estimates for FIR boundary including FL and SSR code at least 10 min prior to entry via VHF, HF or ADS-CPDLC
special_notes: RVSM FL290-FL410: Exclusive RVSM (Bay of Bengal and Arabian Sea); non-exclusive over continental India
FLAS continental India (SAS.1.2.2): WB 0001-1600UTC: A325/A791/N877 FL300/340/360/400; EB FL310/350/390/410. All levels 1601-0000UTC
ADIZ: ADC required before entering Indian airspace from respective FIC, at least 10 min prior to entry. ADC valid between -15 min and +45 min of EOBT

ADS-B OUT mandatory at or above FL290 on PBN routes (L,M,N,P,Q,T designators) and specific routes (A201, A347, A465, A474, A791, B211, B466, G450, R457, R460, R461, W routes) in Indian continental airspace
HF: Delhi Radio frequencies — 10018 kHz (day primary), 5658 kHz (night primary/day secondary), 3467 kHz (night secondary)
ACAS II strongly recommended in RVSM airspace (India)
Fuel state: 'minimum fuel' or 'mayday mayday mayday fuel' ONLY — other phrases not recognized at Indian airports
VIDF.reg_rules:
Airspace (SAS.4.1): Classes A*, B*, C, D, E, F, G. Class A/B over designated controlled airspace (TMA/CTA/CTZ) — IFR only or IFR+VFR. Class C: IFR+VFR, full ATC. *Class A/B implementation under consideration.
VFR Limit (SAS.4.2.1): VFR not permitted above FL150.
RVSM (SAS.1.1): FL290-FL410 (both inclusive) in Delhi, Kolkata, Chennai, Mumbai FIRs. Exclusive RVSM over Bay of Bengal and Arabian Sea oceanic airspace. Non-exclusive over continental India. ACAS II recommended.
FLAS Continental India (SAS.1.2.2): 0001-1600UTC on A325/A791/N877: WB FL300/340/360/400; EB FL310/350/390/410. All levels available 1601-0000UTC for international flights.
FLAS Bay of Bengal (SAS.1.2.1): Various routes — consult table. N877/P628/L759/P570/M300/N563/N571/P574 WB: FL280/320/340/360/380/400; EB: all available (except FL290).
Comm Requirements (SAS.4.3): All flights entering Delhi FIR (except from Mumbai or Kolkata FIR) must pass estimates for FIR boundary (FL + SSR code) at least 10 min prior to entry via VHF, HF or ADS-CPDLC. Delhi Radio HF: 10018 kHz (day), 5658 kHz (night/day secondary), 3467 kHz (night secondary).
ADC/ADIZ (SAS.4.4): Air Defence Clearance required before entering Indian airspace. Obtain from FIC at least 10 min prior to entry. ADC valid -15 min to +45 min EOBT. Valid for entire route including intermediate stops.
ADS-B (SAS.4.8): Mandatory at or above FL290 on PBN routes (L/M/N/P/Q/T designators) and routes A201/A347/A465/A474/A791/B211/B466/G450/R457/R460/R461/W routes in continental India. Indicate B1 or B2 in FPL item 10.
SLOP (SAS.1.11.1): Applicable in oceanic airspace of Chennai, Kolkata and Mumbai FIRs only (beyond TMA/CTA/CTR). Up to 2 NM right of centerline where spacing ≥15 NM; up to 0.5 NM where spacing 6-15 NM.
Fuel State (SAS.4.2.7): ONLY use 'minimum fuel' or 'mayday mayday mayday fuel'. Other phrases not recognized. Inform ATC 10 min before expected diversion.
FPL (SAS.4.5): Submit at least 180 min before EOBT.

VECF.name: Kolkata FIR
VECF.emergency_procedure:
ICAO Standard
・Emergency: Sq7700; declare MAYDAY on current freq or VHF 121.5 MHz.
・Radio Failure (Sq7600):
　Radar env: maintain current FL.
　Non-radar: fly last assigned / expected / cruise FL.
　Blind-broadcast intentions on 121.5 MHz + 123.45 MHz (callsign, FL, position, intention).
・Kolkata FIR specific: Contact Kolkata ACC on assigned VHF; guard 121.5 MHz.
　Fuel emergency phrase: 'MAYDAY MAYDAY MAYDAY FUEL' or 'MINIMUM FUEL' (DGCA CAR SECN 8).
VECF.contingency_procedure:
ICAO Standard
Obtain revised ATC clearance before deviating where possible.
If unable: deviate from track (right preferred).
Broadcast on current freq + 121.5 MHz + 123.45 MHz:
　callsign・FL・position (incl. route/track code)・intention・next reporting point.
・Kolkata FIR: ADC required for ADIZ; 10 min prior coordination required (SAS.4.4).
VECF.cpdlc_procedure:
Kolkata FIR: CPDLC via ADS-CPDLC available
Similar to Delhi FIR: ADS-CPDLC available for boundary reporting.
RNP2/RNP4 + ADS-B OUT + RCP240 aircraft may use CPDLC on designated routes.
VHF primary within radar coverage. HF backup beyond VHF range.
Ref: 2-05 SAS section.
VECF.position_report: Standard IFR position reports; Kolkata ACC also controls portion of Dhaka FIR (L507 AVPOP-ESDOT)
special_notes: Covers Bay of Bengal and eastern India; similar rules to Delhi FIR (SAS.4)
RVSM FL290-FL410: Exclusive RVSM over Bay of Bengal oceanic airspace
FLAS Bay of Bengal (SAS.1.2.1): consult route-specific table. Key: N877/P628/L759/P570/M300/N563/N571/P574 WB FL280/320/340/360/380/400; EB all available (except FL290)
SLOP applicable in oceanic airspace (beyond TMA/CTA/CTR): up to 2 NM right on routes N877/L301/P628/L759/M770/M770A/N895/P646/L507
ADS-B OUT mandatory at or above FL290 on PBN routes in Indian continental airspace
Kolkata ACC provides ATS delegation for L507 AVPOP-ESDOT within Dhaka FIR (FL280-FL460)
ACAS II strongly recommended
FPL submission: at least 180 min before EOBT
VECF.reg_rules:
Airspace: Same classification as other Indian FIRs (SAS.4.1). Classes A*,B*,C,D,E,F,G. VFR not above FL150.
RVSM (SAS.1.1): FL290-FL410 (inclusive). Exclusive RVSM over Bay of Bengal oceanic airspace. ACAS II recommended.
FLAS Bay of Bengal (SAS.1.2.1): N877/P628/L759/P570/M300/N563/N571/P574 WB: FL280/320/340/360/380/400; EB: all available (except FL290). P762/L301/N895/L645 WB: FL320/FL360. P646/L507 WB: all WB levels available. Consult full table.
FLAS Continental India (SAS.1.2.2): 0001-1600UTC: WB FL300/340/360/400; EB FL310/350/390/410. All levels 1601-0000UTC.
SLOP (SAS.1.11.1): Kolkata FIR oceanic airspace (beyond TMA/CTA/CTR): N877/L301/P628/L759/M770/M770A/N895/P646/L507. Up to 2 NM right; 0.5 NM max where spacing 6-15 NM. Inter-pilot frequency 123.45 MHz.
ADS-B (SAS.4.8): Mandatory at or above FL290 on PBN routes in Indian continental airspace. Indicate B1/B2 in FPL item 10.
ADC/ADIZ (SAS.4.4): Air Defence Clearance required. Obtain at least 10 min before entering Indian airspace. Valid -15 to +45 min EOBT.
Dhaka FIR Delegation (SAS.3.4): Kolkata ACC controls L507 AVPOP-ESDOT within Dhaka FIR (FL280-FL460 ATS only; control at or above FL130 remains Kolkata ATCC). Prior approval from CAAB required to operate in this delegated airspace.
Fuel State (SAS.4.2.7): 'minimum fuel' or 'mayday mayday mayday fuel' ONLY.
FPL: Submit at least 180 min before EOBT.

VGFR.name: Dhaka FIR
VGFR.emergency_procedure:
ICAO Standard
・Emergency: Sq7700; declare MAYDAY on current freq or VHF 121.5 MHz.
・Radio Failure (Sq7600):
　Radar env: maintain current FL.
　Non-radar: fly last assigned / expected / cruise FL.
　Blind-broadcast intentions on 121.5 MHz + 123.45 MHz (callsign, FL, position, intention).
・Dhaka FIR specific: Contact Dhaka ACC on VHF; guard 121.5 MHz.
　123.45 MHz air-to-air backup.
VGFR.contingency_procedure:
ICAO Standard
Obtain revised ATC clearance before deviating where possible.
If unable: deviate from track (right preferred).
Broadcast on current freq + 121.5 MHz + 123.45 MHz:
　callsign・FL・position (incl. route/track code)・intention・next reporting point.
・Dhaka FIR: Lateral offset procedures follow ICAO standard; right offset preferred.
VGFR.cpdlc_procedure:
Dhaka FIR: CPDLC not routinely available
VHF-based FIR. CPDLC not mandated. HF backup beyond VHF coverage.
RVSM applicable FL290–FL410. FLAS on A201/B465/A599/L507/G463/R472/R598 (2-05 SAS.1.2.4).
VGFR.position_report: Establish contact with Dhaka ACC/FIC at least 10 min before entering FIR boundary
special_notes: Airspace classes: B, C, D, F, G. IFR required above FL150.
RVSM FL290-FL410 (inclusive); no RVSM transition areas established

ADIZ: Air Defence Clearance (ADC) required before entry. Obtain at least 10 min before crossing ADIZ boundary. Flights on P646/N895/M770/L524/W112 do NOT need ADC unless deviating toward Bangladesh landmass
SLOP applicable on G463 and L507 — 2 NM right only (not 1NM option). In oceanic/remote continental airspace only. Auto offset capability required.
Partial airspace (L507 AVPOP-ESDOT, FL280-FL460) delegated to Kolkata ACC for ATS provision
Longitudinal separation reduced to 10 min on L507/A599/A201/B465/G463/A462/R344/R472/B593/R598
HF: Dhaka Radio 3491/6556/10066/2947 kHz
VGFR.reg_rules:
Airspace (SAS.3.1): Classes B, C, D, F, G. IFR required above FL150 (SAS.3.2.1).
RVSM (SAS.1.1): FL290-FL410 (inclusive). No RVSM transition areas (SAS.1.3.3).
FLAS (SAS.1.2.4): Routes A201/B465/A599/L507/G463/R472/R598: WB FL300/320/340/360/380/400; EB FL290/310/330/350/370/390/410.
Comm (SAS.3.3): Establish radio contact with Dhaka ACC/FIC at least 10 min before entering FIR boundary. Exception: flights from adjacent Indian aerodromes near boundary contact as early as possible but not later than FIR crossing. Dhaka Radio HF: 3491/6556/10066/2947 kHz.
Delegated Airspace (SAS.3.4): L507 between AVPOP and ESDOT (FL280-FL460) — ATS delegated to Kolkata ACC/FIC. Prior approval from Chairman CAAB required. Before entering: contact Dhaka Radio HF or Dhaka ACC 125.7 MHz; provide call sign, departure, destination/ETA, ETO AVPOP/ESDOT.
ADIZ (SAS.3.5): Bangladesh ADIZ covers entire territory plus southern sea area. ADC required at least 10 min before entering ADIZ. Exception: flights on P646/N895/M770/L524/W112 exempt unless deviating toward land mass. If unable to contact ATS, call Air Defence on 124.20 MHz or 6826 kHz.
SLOP (SAS.1.11.2): Applicable on G463 and L507 in oceanic/remote continental airspace. Offset: 2 NM right of centerline only. Auto offset capability required. No ATC notification needed.
Separation (SAS.3.2.2): Longitudinal minimum 15 min reduced to 10 min on L507/A599/A201/B465/G463/A462/R344/R472/B593/R598 for same track/level, crossing track/level, and climb/descent.

KZAK.name: Oakland Oceanic FIR
KZAK.emergency_procedure:
ICAO Standard + Oceanic FIR
・Emergency: Sq7700; declare MAYDAY on current freq or VHF 121.5 MHz.
・Radio Failure (Sq7600):
　Radar env: maintain current FL.
　Non-radar (oceanic): fly last assigned / expected / cruise FL.
　Blind-broadcast on 121.5 MHz + 123.45 MHz: callsign・FL・position・intention.
・Oakland Oceanic: Contact San Francisco ARINC on HF; squawk 7700.
　After radar service terminated, set transponder to code 2000 (if no discrete code assigned).
KZAK.contingency_procedure:
ICAO Standard (oceanic)
Obtain revised ATC clearance before deviating where possible.
If unable: deviate from track (right preferred) and immediately broadcast:
　callsign・FL・position (incl. route/track code)・intention・next reporting point
　on current freq + 121.5 MHz + 123.45 MHz.
Notify ATC of loss of CPDLC/ADS-C or inability to meet RNP.
・Oakland Oceanic: Deviate within 200 NM; notify ATC of loss of CPDLC/ADS-C or RNP4/10.
　Ref: 2-05 PAC.8.2 (Oakland Oceanic PBCS).
KZAK.cpdlc_procedure: ADS-C and CPDLC required for PBCS operations (RNP4/RCP240/RSP180) enabling 30NM longitudinal or 23NM lateral separation. RNP10+CPDLC/ADS-C enables 50NM longitudinal separation.
KZAK.position_report: Position reports at lat/lon coordinates or named fixes per TDM. At Fukuoka/Oakland FIR boundary for eastbound: check on freq with SF ARINC, report altitude and ETO next position (normally 170E). Westbound: transpose beacon code to 2000.
special_notes: RVSM FL290-FL410 (inclusive). FLAS: westbound (180-359°) even FLs FL300-FL400 + odd FL410+; eastbound (000-179°) odd FLs FL290-FL410+
RNP10 required for PACOTS operations. PBCS (RNP4/RCP240/RSP180 + ADS-C/CPDLC) enables 30NM longitudinal or 23NM lateral separation
SLOP: 0 to 2.0 NM right of centerline in 0.1 NM increments. Authorized throughout FAA-controlled oceanic airspace including Oakland Oceanic. No ATC clearance required.
Beacon code: squawk 2000 after radar service terminated upon entering Oakland Oceanic FIR
Mach number: report CPDLC or voice if Mach varies ≥0.02 from entry Mach or last notified change
PACOTS: Oakland issues westbound PACOTS NOTAM (KZAK) by ~1100UTC daily. Westbound tracks C/D/E/F — even FLs FL300-FL400, odd FL410+
PACOTS eastbound tracks 1/2/3/4: odd FLs FL290+. Japan-Hawaii tracks 11/12: odd FLs FL290+
HF contact: San Francisco ARINC. Contact at least 5 min before entering Anchorage FIR from Oakland FIR to get beacon code assignment.
KZAK.reg_rules:
RVSM (PAC.10): FL290-FL410 (inclusive). FLAS: WB (180-359°) even FLs FL300/320/340/360/380/400 + odd FL410+; EB (000-179°) odd FLs FL290/310/330/350/370/390/410+.
Separation (PAC.8.2): 50 NM lateral between RNP10 or RNP4 aircraft. PBCS operations: RNP4+RCP240+RSP180+ADS-C/CPDLC = 30 NM longitudinal; or 23 NM lateral (with waypoint change event and 5 NM deviation event contracts). Advise ATC of CPDLC/ADS-C loss or inability to meet RNP4/10.
PACOTS (PAC.6): RNP10 (or RNP4) required. EB tracks 1/2/3/4 (Japan-NA): odd FLs ≥FL290, crossing 160E 0700-2100UTC. EB tracks 11/12 (Japan-Hawaii): odd FLs ≥FL290, crossing 160E 1000-2100UTC. WB tracks C/D/E/F (NA-Japan): even FL300-FL400, odd ≥FL410, crossing 160E 1900-0800UTC. WB tracks A/B (Hawaii-Japan): even FL300-FL400, odd ≥FL410, crossing 160E 1900-0800UTC. NOTAM KZAK issued ~1100UTC.
SLOP (PAC.10.5): Authorized throughout Oakland Oceanic. 0 to 2.0 NM right of centerline in 0.1 NM increments. No ATC clearance required. Apply outbound after reaching cruise FL; retain until top of descent. Aircraft departing Hawaii: apply SLOP within 70 NM of entering Oakland Oceanic.
Beacon Code (PAC.1.3): Squawk 2000 after radar service terminated upon entering Oakland Oceanic FIR. Westbounds: ANC ARTCC assigns code 2000 at ANC/Tokyo FIR boundary, or change to 2000 when crossing 164E.
Mach (PAC.4): ATC approved Mach included in clearance. Report if Mach varies ≥0.02 from entry Mach or last notified speed change (voice or CPDLC).
Position Reports (PAC.2): At lat/lon or named fixes per TDM. Random routes: every 5° or 10° longitude (E-W flights) or latitude (N-S flights). Address to San Francisco ARINC when entering Oakland/Anchorage FIR.
HF / CPDLC: Contact San Francisco ARINC on HF. CPDLC required for PBCS operations.

PAZAO.name: Anchorage Oceanic FIR (Western Sector)
PAZAO.emergency_procedure:
ICAO Standard + Oceanic FIR
・Emergency: Sq7700; declare MAYDAY on current freq or VHF 121.5 MHz.
・Radio Failure (Sq7600):
　Radar env: maintain current FL.
　Non-radar (oceanic): fly last assigned / expected / cruise FL.
　Blind-broadcast on 121.5 MHz + 123.45 MHz: callsign・FL・position・intention.
・Anchorage Oceanic (W): Contact Anchorage ARTCC on HF; squawk 7700.
　If no discrete code assigned, squawk 2000.
PAZAO.contingency_procedure:
ICAO Standard (oceanic)
Obtain revised ATC clearance before deviating where possible.
If unable: deviate from track (right preferred) and immediately broadcast:
　callsign・FL・position (incl. route/track code)・intention・next reporting point
　on current freq + 121.5 MHz + 123.45 MHz.
Notify ATC of loss of CPDLC/ADS-C or inability to meet RNP.
・Anchorage Oceanic: Deviate within 200 NM; notify ATC of loss of CPDLC/ADS-C or RNP.
　Ref: 2-05 PAC.8.3 (Anchorage Oceanic PBCS).
PAZAO.cpdlc_procedure: ADS-C and CPDLC required for PBCS operations (RNP4/RCP240/RSP180) enabling 30NM longitudinal or 23NM lateral separation. RNP10+ADS-C/CPDLC enables 50NM longitudinal separation.
PAZAO.position_report: Address position reports to San Francisco ARINC when entering Anchorage/Oakland FIR. Contact ANC ARTCC on VHF 129.4 for NOPAC HF frequencies and SELCAL verification.
special_notes: RVSM FL290-FL410 (inclusive). Same FLAS as Oakland: WB even FL300-FL400 + odd FL410+; EB odd FL290-FL410+
50 NM lateral separation between RNP10 or RNP4 aircraft (PAC.8.3.1)
PBCS: RNP4+RCP240+RSP180+ADS-C/CPDLC = 30 NM longitudinal or 23 NM lateral separation
SLOP: authorized throughout Anchorage Oceanic FIR. 0 to 2.0 NM right of centerline in 0.1 NM increments. No ATC clearance required.
SLOP also applies in Anchorage Arctic FIR and portions of Anchorage Domestic FIR more than 12 miles offshore or west of 160W on Alaska Peninsula
NOPAC routes: R220/R580 (eastbound), M523 (westbound), A590 (eastbound). RNP4+ADS-C+CPDLC+RCP240+RSP180 required for M523 and FL334-FL400 on R220/R580
Mach: report if varies ≥0.02 from entry Mach or last notified change (voice or CPDLC) — applies south of N63 and west of W165 in Anchorage Domestic FIR, and throughout Anchorage Oceanic FIR

Beacon code: contact SF ARINC on HF at least 5 min before entering Anchorage FIR from Oakland FIR for code assignment. Eastbound: ANC assigns discrete code 150 NM west of PINSO/SHEMYA/CHIPT. Westbound: squawk 2000 at ANC/Tokyo FIR boundary or at 164E.
PAZAO.reg_rules:
RVSM (PAC.10): FL290-FL410 (inclusive). FLAS: WB (180-359°) even FL300/320/340/360/380/400 + odd FL410+; EB (000-179°) odd FL290/310/330/350/370/390/410+.
Separation (PAC.8.3): 50 NM lateral between RNP10 or RNP4 aircraft. PBCS: RNP4+RCP240+RSP180+ADS-C/CPDLC = 30 NM longitudinal; 23 NM lateral (with ADS-C waypoint change event and 5 NM deviation event contracts). ADS-C CDP available (15 NM or 25 NM longitudinal during climb/descent).
NOPAC Routes (PAC.5): R220 (westbound) and R580 (eastbound): RNP10 minimum; RNP4+ADS-C+CPDLC+RCP240+RSP180 required for FL334-FL400 (otherwise file at/below FL330 or at/above FL410). M523 (westbound): RNP4+ADS-C+CPDLC+RCP240+RSP180 mandatory, FL340-FL400. A590 (eastbound): RNP10 required FL290-FL410. Contact ANC ARTCC on VHF 129.4 for HF frequencies and SELCAL before entering NOPAC.
SLOP (PAC.10.5): Authorized throughout Anchorage Oceanic FIR, Arctic FIR, and portions of Domestic FIR (>12 NM offshore or over Alaska Peninsula west of 160W). 0 to 2.0 NM right in 0.1 NM increments. No ATC clearance required.
Mach (PAC.4): ATC-approved Mach in clearance. Report if varies ≥0.02 from entry Mach or last notified speed (voice or CPDLC). Applies south of N63/west of W165 in ANC Domestic FIR and throughout ANC Oceanic FIR.
Beacon Code (PAC.1.3/1.4): Eastbound: ANC assigns discrete code ~150 NM west of entry. Contact SF ARINC HF at least 5 min before entering ANC FIR from Oakland FIR. Westbound: squawk 2000 at ANC/Tokyo boundary or at 164E.
Position Reports (PAC.2): Address to San Francisco ARINC when entering Anchorage/Oakland FIR. At lat/lon or named fixes per TDM. Every 5° or 10° on random routes.

PAZAU.name: Anchorage Oceanic FIR (Eastern Sector)
PAZAU.emergency_procedure:
ICAO Standard + Oceanic FIR
・Emergency: Sq7700; declare MAYDAY on current freq or VHF 121.5 MHz.
・Radio Failure (Sq7600):
　Radar env: maintain current FL.
　Non-radar (oceanic): fly last assigned / expected / cruise FL.
　Blind-broadcast on 121.5 MHz + 123.45 MHz: callsign・FL・position・intention.
・Anchorage Oceanic (E): Contact Anchorage ARTCC on HF; squawk 7700.
　If no discrete code assigned, squawk 2000.
PAZAU.contingency_procedure:
ICAO Standard (oceanic)
Obtain revised ATC clearance before deviating where possible.
If unable: deviate from track (right preferred) and immediately broadcast:
　callsign・FL・position (incl. route/track code)・intention・next reporting point
　on current freq + 121.5 MHz + 123.45 MHz.
Notify ATC of loss of CPDLC/ADS-C or inability to meet RNP.
・Anchorage Oceanic: Deviate within 200 NM; notify ATC of loss of CPDLC/ADS-C or RNP.
　Ref: 2-05 PAC.8.3 (Anchorage Oceanic PBCS).
PAZAU.cpdlc_procedure: ADS-C and CPDLC required for PBCS operations (RNP4/RCP240/RSP180) enabling 30NM longitudinal or 23NM lateral separation.
PAZAU.position_report: Address position reports to San Francisco ARINC when entering Anchorage/Oakland FIR.
special_notes: Same rules as PAZAO (Western Sector) — same Anchorage Oceanic FIR separated for database purposes
RVSM FL290-FL410; 50 NM lateral separation (RNP10/RNP4); PBCS 30NM/23NM with ADS-C/CPDLC
SLOP: 0 to 2.0 NM right, 0.1 NM increments, no ATC clearance required
NOPAC routes and PACOTS procedures apply — see PAZAO for details
Mach: report if varies ≥0.02 from entry or last notified speed change
PAZAU.reg_rules:
Note: PAZAU (Eastern Sector) operates under identical rules to PAZAO (Western Sector) — both are within the Anchorage Oceanic FIR managed by Anchorage ARTCC.
RVSM (PAC.10): FL290-FL410 (inclusive). FLAS: WB even FL300/320/340/360/380/400 + odd FL410+; EB odd FL290/310/330/350/370/390/410+.
Separation (PAC.8.3): 50 NM lateral (RNP10/RNP4). PBCS: RNP4+RCP240+RSP180+ADS-C/CPDLC = 30 NM longitudinal; 23 NM lateral. ADS-C CDP available for altitude changes.
SLOP (PAC.10.5): 0 to 2.0 NM right in 0.1 NM increments. No ATC clearance required. Applies throughout Anchorage Oceanic FIR.
Mach (PAC.4): Report if varies ≥0.02 from entry or last notified speed change (voice or CPDLC).
NOPAC / PACOTS: Refer to PAZAO entry for full details on NOPAC routes (R220/M523/R580/A590) and PACOTS.
Position Reports (PAC.2): Address to San Francisco ARINC. At lat/lon coordinates or named fixes per TDM.

PHZH.name: Honolulu FIR
PHZH.emergency_procedure:
ICAO Standard + Oceanic FIR
・Emergency: Sq7700; declare MAYDAY on current freq or VHF 121.5 MHz.
・Radio Failure (Sq7600):
　Radar env: maintain current FL.
　Non-radar (oceanic): fly last assigned / expected / cruise FL.
　Blind-broadcast on 121.5 MHz + 123.45 MHz: callsign・FL・position・intention.
・Honolulu FIR: Contact Honolulu Control Facility (HCF) on HF or guard 121.5 MHz; squawk 7700.
PHZH.contingency_procedure:
ICAO Standard (oceanic)
Obtain revised ATC clearance before deviating where possible.
If unable: deviate from track (right preferred) and immediately broadcast:
　callsign・FL・position (incl. route/track code)・intention・next reporting point
　on current freq + 121.5 MHz + 123.45 MHz.
Notify ATC of loss of CPDLC/ADS-C or inability to meet RNP.
・Honolulu FIR: Deviate within 200 NM; notify ATC of loss of CPDLC/ADS-C or RNP.
　Same standards as Oakland Oceanic (2-05 PAC.8.2).
PHZH.cpdlc_procedure: ADS-C and CPDLC required for PBCS operations enabling reduced separation. Same standards as Oakland Oceanic (RNP10 = 50NM; RNP4 = 30NM longitudinal or 23NM lateral).
PHZH.position_report: Address reports to San Francisco ARINC when entering Oakland/Anchorage FIR. HCF is gateway for Japan-Hawaii PACOTS tracks 11/12 and Hawaii-Japan tracks A/B.
special_notes: Honolulu Control Facility (HCF) manages airspace around Hawaii
RVSM FL290-FL410 (same as Oakland Oceanic — FAA-controlled oceanic airspace)
SLOP authorized throughout HCF airspace (PAC.10.5). 0 to 2.0 NM right in 0.1 NM increments. No ATC clearance required.
Arriving Hawaii: discontinue SLOP no later than 70 NM after entering HCF airspace or when receiving radar vectors, whichever first. Hawaii inter-island flights must NOT use SLOP.
Departing Hawaii: apply SLOP upon reaching initial cruise FL within 70 NM of entering Oakland Oceanic
PACOTS Japan-Hawaii Tracks: EB tracks 11/12 cross 160E 1000-2100UTC (1200-1600UTC). WB tracks A/B cross 160E 1900-0800UTC (2300-0600UTC). Even FLs FL300-FL400, odd FL410+
Hawaiian gateway waypoints: THOMA/DANNO/CANON/LILIA/PUPPI/SYVAD/HOOPA (HCF boundary)
UPR (User Preferred Routes): available between Hawaii and Asia. Must connect to/from OTR gateway fixes at Hawaii end.
PHZH.reg_rules:
RVSM (PAC.10): FL290-FL410 (inclusive). FAA-controlled oceanic airspace. FLAS: WB even FL300/320/340/360/380/400 + odd FL410+; EB odd FL290/310/330/350/370/390/410+.
SLOP (PAC.10.5): Authorized throughout Honolulu Control Facility (HCF) airspace. 0 to 2.0 NM right in 0.1 NM increments. No ATC clearance required. Arriving Hawaii: discontinue SLOP within 70 NM after entering HCF or on radar vectors. Departing Hawaii: apply within 70 NM before entering Oakland Oceanic. Inter-island flights: SLOP prohibited.
Separation: Same as Oakland Oceanic (PAC.8.2). RNP10 = 50 NM lateral; PBCS (RNP4+RCP240+RSP180+ADS-C/CPDLC) = 30 NM longitudinal or 23 NM lateral.
PACOTS Japan-Hawaii (PAC.6.3): EB tracks 11/12: cross 160E 1000-2100UTC (target crossing 1200-1600UTC). Odd cardinal FLs ≥FL290. WB tracks A/B: cross 160E 1900-0800UTC (target 2300-0600UTC). Even FL300-FL400 or odd FL410+. NOTAM KZAK issued ~1100UTC.
Hawaiian Gateway Waypoints: THOMA/DANNO/CANON/LILIA/PUPPI/SYVAD (→BOOKE PHNL); HOOPA (→KATHS OPACA PHNL).
UPR Asia-Hawaii (PAC.7.2): EB: connect to OTR gateway (ADNAP OTR5/7, AVBET OTR9/11, LAPIL OTR13, POVAL OTR15, PIPIK OTR17, DAGDA G223). End at Hawaiian gateway waypoint on HCF boundary. WB: start from Hawaiian gateway waypoint. If crossing 160E 1100-1230UTC, stay ≥50 NM south of PACOTS TRACK 2.

CZVR.name: Vancouver FIR
CZVR.emergency_procedure:
ICAO Standard
・Emergency: Sq7700; declare MAYDAY on current freq or VHF 121.5 MHz.
・Radio Failure (Sq7600):
　Radar env: maintain current FL.
　Non-radar: fly last assigned / expected / cruise FL.
　Blind-broadcast intentions on 121.5 MHz + 123.45 MHz (callsign, FL, position, intention).
・Vancouver FIR specific: Contact Vancouver ACC on VHF; guard 121.5 MHz.
　SELCAL monitoring required for oceanic transition to Oakland/Anchorage FIR.
CZVR.contingency_procedure:
ICAO Standard
Obtain revised ATC clearance before deviating where possible.
If unable: deviate from track (right preferred).
Broadcast on current freq + 121.5 MHz + 123.45 MHz:
　callsign・FL・position (incl. route/track code)・intention・next reporting point.
・Vancouver FIR: Full radar coverage; ATC will vector conflicting traffic. Immediate ATC contact required.
CZVR.cpdlc_procedure:
Vancouver FIR (NAV CANADA): CPDLC DCL available
CPDLC Departure Clearance (DCL) available at designated Canadian airports.
All JAL long-haul aircraft (787-8/-9, 777-200ER/-300ER, A350-1000) are CPDLC-equipped.
VHF radar coverage throughout Vancouver FIR (Class A/B/C/D/E airspace).
Oceanic transition to Oakland/Anchorage FIR uses SELCAL/HF.
Ref: 2-05 NAM.1.4.1 (CPDLC DCL procedure).
CZVR.position_report: Position reports required. POWOL/YAZ to CYVR for Pacific arrivals.
special_notes: RVSM FL290-FL410 (inclusive) — Canadian RVSM same stratum as US (NAM.2.15)
8.33 kHz frequency spacing NOT required in Canada
CMNPS (Canadian Minimum Navigation Performance Specifications) airspace in ACA/NCA and portions of SCA (NAM.2.11)
Southern Control Area (SCA), Northern Control Area (NCA), Arctic Control Area (ACA) — different airspace designations
HF: Arctic Radio/Gander Radio on HF in northern Canada (NAM.2.11.6); SATCOM acceptable alternative to HF. VHF 126.7 MHz or HF 5,680/6,706/11,232 kHz
PACOTS Pacific gateway routes: POWOL CYVR or YAZ CYVR for Vancouver-bound Pacific traffic
ADS-B OUT required in Canadian Class A, B, C airspace and Class E above 10,000 ft MSL
CZVR.reg_rules:
Airspace (NAM.2.1): Canadian Domestic Airspace (CDA) divided into Southern Control Area (SCA), Northern Control Area (NCA), and Arctic Control Area (ACA). Class A: 18,000 ft ASL and above (IFR only). Class B: 12,500-18,000 ft ASL (IFR + CVFR). Class C-G: lower levels.
RVSM (NAM.2.15): FL290-FL410 (inclusive). FLAS: WB (180-359°) FL300/320/340/360/380/400; EB (000-179°) FL290/310/330/350/370/390/410. Non-RVSM: WB FL310/350/390/430; EB FL270/310(add)/350/390/450.
8.33 kHz: NOT required — Canada does not mandate 8.33 kHz frequency spacing.
CMNPS Airspace (NAM.2.11): Designated within ACA, NCA and portions of SCA. Requires CMNPS-certified navigation equipment. RNAV routes, DME distance requirements apply per route.
Communications (NAM.2.11.6): VHF for routine; HF (Arctic Radio or Gander Radio) in northern Canada. SATCOM voice is acceptable alternative. Frequency: VHF 126.7 MHz or HF 5,680/6,706/11,232 kHz. If unable HF/SATCOM, make position reports within ~200 NM of any VHF facility.
PACOTS Gateway (PAC.7.5): Pacific arrivals to Vancouver: POWOL CYVR or YAZ CYVR.
ADS-B (NAM.1.1.5): Required in Class A, B, C airspace and Class E at or above 10,000 ft MSL. Indicate in FPL.
ADIZ (NAM.2.1.7): Canadian ADIZ applies in designated areas. DVFR flight plan required for VFR flights entering ADIZ.

ZBPE.name: Beijing FIR
ZBPE.emergency_procedure:
ICAO Standard
・Emergency: Sq7700; declare MAYDAY on current freq or VHF 121.5 MHz.
・Radio Failure (Sq7600):
　Radar env: maintain current FL.
　Non-radar: fly last assigned / expected / cruise FL.
　Blind-broadcast intentions on 121.5 MHz + 123.45 MHz (callsign, FL, position, intention).
・Beijing FIR specific: Contact Beijing ACC on VHF; guard 121.5 MHz or 123.45 MHz.
　Blind-transmit: ALL freq + 121.5 + 123.45 MHz: 'TRANSMITTING BLIND [callsign] [intent]'.
ZBPE.contingency_procedure:
ICAO Standard
Obtain revised ATC clearance before deviating where possible.
If unable: deviate from track (right preferred).
Broadcast on current freq + 121.5 MHz + 123.45 MHz:
　callsign・FL・position (incl. route/track code)・intention・next reporting point.
・Beijing FIR: Contingency descent – notify ATC; TCAS monitor; broadcast blind if ATC contact lost.
　Ref: 2-05 PRC.7.4.6 Contingency Actions.
ZBPE.cpdlc_procedure:
Beijing FIR: CPDLC limited (not routinely available)
PRC CPDLC is designated primarily for western China routes (L888, Y1, Y2 in Lanzhou/Urumqi area).
Beijing FIR (domestic routes) uses VHF voice as primary communication.
No CPDLC mandate for Beijing FIR en-route operations.
Blind transmission on 121.5 MHz if communication lost. Ref: 2-03 §2.2.15 (CPDLC-PRC).
ZBPE.position_report: Report on entry to/exit from China FIRs; standard IFR position reports on ATS routes
special_notes: Metric flight levels — ATC clears in meters; pilot flies using feet altimeter with China RVSM conversion table
RVSM stratum: 8,900 m (FL291) to 12,500 m (FL411) inclusive
SLOP: in non-radar airspace only — 1 or 2 NM right of centreline. In radar airspace, requires ATC approval. Auto offset capability required.
ADS-B: mandatory in ADS-B control airspace (ACC/APP where radar not available from Dec 2020). Indicate B1/B2 in FPL. Waiver required if not equipped.
FLAS FL transition: at FIR boundary (Beijing ACC - adjacent ACC), transition positions notified on charts (e.g. B339/W33/A310 at POLHO)
PRC.7.2: AKARA-FUKUE Corridor and AKARA-APITO-MIKES Route procedures apply for certain overflights
ZBPE.reg_rules:
Metric FLs (PRC.7.4.4): ATC issues clearances in meters. Pilot uses China RVSM conversion table to determine corresponding FL in feet and flies in feet. Rounding difference never more than 30 m.
RVSM (PRC.7.4.1): 8,900 m (FL291) to 12,500 m (FL411) inclusive. 300 m (1,000 ft) vertical separation between RVSM-approved aircraft.
FLAS (PRC.7.4.3): EB (true track 0-179°): 12500m(FL411)/11900m(FL391)/11300m(FL371)/10700m(FL351)/10100m(FL331)/9500m(FL311)/8900m(FL291). WB (true track 180-359°): 12200m(FL401)/11600m(FL381)/11000m(FL361)/10400m(FL341)/9800m(FL321)/9200m(FL301).
FL Transition (PRC.7.4.7): At FIR boundary entering Chinese airspace, transition from feet to metric FL per ATC instruction. Transition positions marked on route charts (☆ symbol). E.g. B339/W33/A310 at POLHO (Ulaanbaatar ACC - Beijing ACC): 27 NM east of POLHO. A599 at LINSO (Yangon ACC - Kunming ACC): 40.5 NM west of GULOT.
SLOP (PRC.7.4.5): Applicable in non-radar airspace only — 1 or 2 NM right of centreline. Auto offset capability required. No ATC notification in non-radar airspace. In radar airspace: ATC approval required before applying or continuing offset.
ADS-B (PRC.5): Mandatory in ADS-B control airspace (ACC/APP areas without radar from Dec 31, 2020). Indicate B1/B2 in FPL item 10. Non-equipped aircraft require waiver from CAAC Operations Management Center (ATMB).
Contingency (PRC.7.4.6): Deviation for level change: turn 30° right, track 20 km out (5 NM right), fly parallel original route, climb/descent, return. Broadcast on 121.5 MHz and 123.45 MHz. Illuminate exterior lights.

ZHWH.name: Wuhan FIR
ZHWH.emergency_procedure:
ICAO Standard
・Emergency: Sq7700; declare MAYDAY on current freq or VHF 121.5 MHz.
・Radio Failure (Sq7600):
　Radar env: maintain current FL.
　Non-radar: fly last assigned / expected / cruise FL.
　Blind-broadcast intentions on 121.5 MHz + 123.45 MHz (callsign, FL, position, intention).
・Wuhan FIR specific: Contact Wuhan ACC on VHF or 121.5 MHz; 123.45 MHz backup.
　Blind-transmit on all assigned freq + 121.5 + 123.45 MHz if radio failure.
ZHWH.contingency_procedure:
ICAO Standard
Obtain revised ATC clearance before deviating where possible.
If unable: deviate from track (right preferred).
Broadcast on current freq + 121.5 MHz + 123.45 MHz:
　callsign・FL・position (incl. route/track code)・intention・next reporting point.
・Wuhan FIR: Contingency – notify ATC; descend by ±500 ft then ±1000 ft if needed; TCAS monitor.
　Ref: 2-05 PRC.7.4.6.
ZHWH.cpdlc_procedure:
Wuhan FIR: CPDLC not routinely available
VHF-based domestic FIR. CPDLC not mandated. PRC CPDLC applies only on
western routes (L888, Y1, Y2); not applicable in Wuhan FIR.
Primary: VHF voice. Blind transmission on 121.5 + 123.45 MHz if contact lost.
ZHWH.position_report: Standard IFR position reports on ATS routes; report on entry/exit from China FIRs
special_notes: Same rules as ZBPE (Beijing FIR) — all China FIRs use identical RVSM/FLAS/SLOP/ADS-B regulations
Metric flight levels: ATC clears in meters; pilot flies in feet using China RVSM conversion table
RVSM: 8,900 m (FL291) to 12,500 m (FL411) inclusive
SLOP: 1 or 2 NM right in non-radar airspace only; ATC approval required in radar airspace
ADS-B mandatory in ADS-B control airspace
ZHWH.reg_rules:
Metric FLs (PRC.7.4.4): ATC clears in meters; pilot flies in feet using China RVSM conversion table. Difference never more than 30 m.
RVSM (PRC.7.4.1): 8,900 m (FL291) to 12,500 m (FL411) inclusive. 300 m (1,000 ft) VSM between RVSM-approved aircraft.
FLAS (PRC.7.4.3): EB (true track 0-179°): FL411/391/371/351/331/311/291. WB (true track 180-359°): FL401/381/361/341/321/301.
FL Transition: At FIR boundary, transition from feet to metric FL per ATC instruction at positions marked on route charts.
SLOP (PRC.7.4.5): Non-radar airspace: 1 or 2 NM right only. Radar airspace: ATC approval required. Auto offset capability required.
ADS-B (PRC.5): Mandatory in ADS-B control airspace (ACC/APP without radar). B1/B2 in FPL item 10. Waiver needed if not equipped.
Contingency (PRC.7.4.6): Level change: 30° right, 20 km out (5 NM right of centreline), parallel route, climb/descend, return. Broadcast on 121.5 MHz (backup: 123.45 MHz). Illuminate exterior lights.

ZPKM.name: Kunming FIR
ZPKM.emergency_procedure:
ICAO Standard
・Emergency: Sq7700; declare MAYDAY on current freq or VHF 121.5 MHz.
・Radio Failure (Sq7600):
　Radar env: maintain current FL.
　Non-radar: fly last assigned / expected / cruise FL.
　Blind-broadcast intentions on 121.5 MHz + 123.45 MHz (callsign, FL, position, intention).
・Kunming FIR specific: Contact Kunming ACC on VHF or 121.5 / 123.45 MHz.
　Blind-transmit on all assigned freq + 121.5 + 123.45 MHz if radio failure.
ZPKM.contingency_procedure:
ICAO Standard
Obtain revised ATC clearance before deviating where possible.
If unable: deviate from track (right preferred).
Broadcast on current freq + 121.5 MHz + 123.45 MHz:
　callsign・FL・position (incl. route/track code)・intention・next reporting point.
・Kunming FIR: Contingency – notify ATC; TCAS monitor; broadcast blind if ATC contact lost.
　Ref: 2-05 PRC.7.4.6.
ZPKM.cpdlc_procedure:
Kunming FIR: CPDLC limited availability
PRC CPDLC available on designated L888 route (KADKI–XKC, Lanzhou/Urumqi area).
Kunming FIR (domestic/China–SE Asia routes) primarily VHF voice.
Specific CPDLC availability depends on route; confirm pre-flight.
Ref: 2-03 §2.2.15 (CPDLC-PRC).
ZPKM.position_report: Standard IFR position reports on ATS routes; report on entry/exit from China FIRs
special_notes: Same rules as ZBPE (Beijing FIR) — all China FIRs use identical RVSM/FLAS/SLOP/ADS-B regulations
Key FIR boundary: A599 at LINSO (Yangon ACC - Kunming ACC) — FLAS transition at 40.5 NM west of GULOT
Metric flight levels: ATC clears in meters; pilot flies in feet using China RVSM conversion table

ZPKM.reg_rules:
Metric FLs (PRC.7.4.4): ATC clears in meters; pilot flies in feet using China RVSM conversion table. Difference never more than 30 m.
RVSM (PRC.7.4.1): 8,900 m (FL291) to 12,500 m (FL411) inclusive. 300 m (1,000 ft) VSM between RVSM-approved aircraft.
FLAS (PRC.7.4.3): EB (true track 0-179°): FL411/391/371/351/331/311/291. WB (true track 180-359°): FL401/381/361/341/321/301.
FL Transition at A599/LINSO (PRC.7.4.7.2): EB (entering China): at 40.5 NM west of GULOT (☆) — FL290→8900m/FL310→9500m/FL330→10100m/FL350→10700m/FL370→11300m/FL390→11900m/FL410→12500m. WB (exiting China): at 13.5 NM west of GULOT — reverse conversion. FLAS transition also at R474/TEBAK (Nanning ACC - Hanoi ACC).
SLOP (PRC.7.4.5): Non-radar airspace: 1 or 2 NM right only. Radar airspace: ATC approval required. Auto offset capability required.
ADS-B (PRC.5): Mandatory in ADS-B control airspace. B1/B2 in FPL item 10.
Contingency (PRC.7.4.6): Level change: 30° right, 20 km out (5 NM right), parallel, climb/descend, return. Broadcast on 121.5 MHz and 123.45 MHz.

ZYSH.name: Shenyang FIR
ZYSH.emergency_procedure:
ICAO Standard
・Emergency: Sq7700; declare MAYDAY on current freq or VHF 121.5 MHz.
・Radio Failure (Sq7600):
　Radar env: maintain current FL.
　Non-radar: fly last assigned / expected / cruise FL.
　Blind-broadcast intentions on 121.5 MHz + 123.45 MHz (callsign, FL, position, intention).
・Shenyang FIR specific: Contact Shenyang ACC on VHF or 121.5 / 123.45 MHz.
　Blind-transmit on all assigned freq + 121.5 + 123.45 MHz if radio failure.
ZYSH.contingency_procedure:
ICAO Standard
Obtain revised ATC clearance before deviating where possible.
If unable: deviate from track (right preferred).
Broadcast on current freq + 121.5 MHz + 123.45 MHz:
　callsign・FL・position (incl. route/track code)・intention・next reporting point.
・Shenyang FIR: Contingency – notify ATC; TCAS monitor; broadcast blind if ATC contact lost.
　Ref: 2-05 PRC.7.4.6.
ZYSH.cpdlc_procedure:
Shenyang FIR: CPDLC not routinely available
VHF-based domestic FIR. CPDLC not mandated in Shenyang FIR.
PRC CPDLC applies only on western routes (L888, Y1, Y2); not applicable here.
Primary: VHF voice. Blind transmission on 121.5 + 123.45 MHz if contact lost.
ZYSH.position_report: Standard IFR position reports on ATS routes; report on entry/exit from China FIRs

ZYSH.reg_rules:

RVSM (PRC.7.4.1): 8,900 m (FL291) to 12,500 m (FL411) inclusive. 300 m (1,000 ft) VSM between RVSM-approved aircraft.

EB (exiting China to Incheon): at GONAV — reverse conversion (FL291→FL290, FL311→FL310, etc.). Transition also at A593/SADLI (Shanghai ACC - Fukuoka ACC): EB FL250/290/310/390, WB FL240/280/300/400.

YBBB.name: Brisbane FIR
YBBB.cpdlc_procedure:
Brisbane FIR: CPDLC available
CPDLC (DCL) available at major Australian airports and en-route in designated areas.
RNP4 + CPDLC/ADS-C enables reduced oceanic separation on designated routes.
HF required in remote/oceanic areas beyond VHF coverage. SELCAL monitoring recommended.
Logon to Brisbane Centre (YBBB) for CPDLC clearances.
YBBB.emergency_procedure:
ICAO Standard
・Emergency: Sq7700; declare MAYDAY on current freq or VHF 121.5 MHz.
・Radio Failure (Sq7600):
　Radar env: maintain current FL.
　Non-radar: fly last assigned / expected / cruise FL.
　Blind-broadcast on 121.5 MHz + 123.45 MHz (callsign, FL, position, intention).
・Brisbane FIR specific: Contact Brisbane Centre on assigned VHF; guard 121.5 MHz.
　HF: Brisbane Radio (primary/secondary frequencies per sector). SELCAL if equipped.
YBBB.contingency_procedure:
ICAO Standard
Obtain revised ATC clearance before deviating where possible.
If unable: deviate from track (right preferred).
Broadcast on current freq + 121.5 MHz + 123.45 MHz:
　callsign・FL・position (incl. route/track code)・intention・next reporting point.
・Brisbane FIR: Deviate right of track; notify Brisbane Centre.
　123.45 MHz air-to-air coordination for conflicting traffic.
YBBB.position_report: Not required in radar-covered domestic airspace. Required in non-radar/oceanic areas at waypoints.
YBBB.special_notes: RVSM airspace FL290-FL410. HF required in remote/oceanic areas. CPDLC available at major airports and en-route.
YBBB.reg_rules:
Brisbane FIR (YBBB)
・RVSM適用空域: FL290〜FL410
・RNP10航路にはHF通信必須（VHF圏外）
・CPDLC available on designated routes; DCL at major airports
・ADS-B搭載機は監視対応空域でSPAD防止のため承認高度維持
Ref: AIP Australia ENR 1.6, 2-05 context
